paper_title: The Potential Effects and Use of Chinese Herbal Medicine Pine Pollen (Pinus pollen): A Bibliometric Analysis of Pharmacological and Clinical Studies

methods.description: This method describes the process used to systematically extract relevant data from scientific articles concerning the pharmacological and clinical studies of Chinese Herbal Medicine Pine Pollen for a bibliometric analysis. It involves developing a structured form, defining classification criteria, performing extraction by trained personnel, verifying the data, and resolving discrepancies.

steps.description: Two authors (SBL and NL) developed a structured data extraction form.
steps.searchable_summary: Develop a structured data extraction form.
inputs: Requirements for data points (publication info, research topics/diseases, usage/preparations, conclusions, adverse events)

steps.description: Conclusions were classified as effective, ineffective, or unclear. "Effective" was defined as whether the study achieved its objective and statistically favored pine pollen; "ineffective" -The study did not achieve its objective or was not significant for pine pollen; "unclear" -The author did not provide a clear conclusion.
steps.searchable_summary: Define criteria for classifying study conclusions (effective, ineffective, unclear).

steps.description: Five authors (SBL, NL, FLB, BYL, and YPZ) performed data extraction. All were trained in the standard and skills of data extraction. The following data were extracted: (1) publication information, including study ID, first author of the article, year of publication, study design, and language; (2) pharmacological research topics investigated based on Pharmacology (8 th ed.) [14] or diseases investigated according to the International Statistical Classification of Diseases and Related Health Problems 10 th Revision (ICD-10) (http://apps.who.int/classifications/icd10/browse/2010/en); (3) usage and preparations of pine pollen; (4) conclusions, classified as effective, ineffective, or unclear; (5) adverse events or adverse effects in clinical studies.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract specified data points from scientific articles using the structured form and defined criteria.
outputs: Raw extracted data (e.g., in a spreadsheet or database)

steps.description: Extracted data were verified by SBL.
steps.searchable_summary: Verify the accuracy and consistency of the extracted data.

steps.description: Any discrepancies were discussed with a third author.
steps.searchable_summary: Discuss and resolve discrepancies found during data verification.
